mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving higher corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce a positive 60-day return frequency above 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and inflationary pressure, prompting further central bank rate hikes that compress equity multiples and yield negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening periods show no statistically significant difference in 20-day return distributions compared to baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms macroeconomic expansion, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and investor confidence to produce positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters yield a 5-day forward return frequency below 50% over a 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals accelerating inflation that forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI spikes above the threshold consistently precede positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in > 65% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and corporate profitability to drive positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to produce a positive 20-day return frequency above 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Surging retail sales during a late-cycle expansion phase exhausts consumer balance sheets and accelerates inflation expectations, prompting central bank hawkishness that immediately depresses equity valuations and yields negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: High retail sales growth in late-cycle regimes consistently precedes positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in > 60% of cases.
